project.title: DHFR2 Proteomic analysis - LC MS/MS NICB DCU
project.projectDescription: LC-MS/MS shotgun proteomic analysis looking at DHFR2 in mammalian cell lines.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: In-solution tryptic digestion using FASP sample preparation. LC-MS/MS using C18 peptide nanoflow separation into an Orbitrap Fusion Tribrid MS instrument.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: MS data analysed using SEQUEST HT in proteome discoverer against human fasta sequence from UniProt KB

references.referenceLine: Drago P, Bookey N, Leung KY, Henry M, Meleady P, Greene NDE, Parle-McDermott A. DHFR2 RNA directly regulates dihydrofolate reductase and its expression level impacts folate one carbon metabolism. FASEB J. 2025 39(4):e70391

Characteristics[CellLine]: HepG2 cells were purchased from the American Type Culture Collection.
Characteristics[CleavageAgent]: Trypsin was set as the digestion enzyme for all samples.
Comment[Instrument]: Dionex Ultimate 3000 Reversed‐phase Capillary high‐pressure Liquid Chromatography (RSCLC) nanosystem coupled to a hybrid linear Ion‐trap/Orbitrap Fusion Tribrid mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific, Dublin, Ireland).
Comment[AcquisitionMethod]: Data‐dependent acquisition (DDA) and targeted‐dependent acquisition (TDA) LC–MS/MS analysis
Comment[GradientTime]: separated for 120 min at 300 nL/min for data‐dependent analysis and 60 min at 300 nL/min for targeted MS analysis.
Comment[FragmentationMethod]: Fragmentation of precursor ions was carried out using higher energy collision‐induced dissociation (HCD) at 28%.
Comment[MS2MassAnalyzer]: the resulting MS/MS ions were detected via a linear ion trap
the resulting MS/MS ions were detected via the orbitrap analyzer at 30 000 resolution at 200 m/z.
Comment[PrecursorMassTolerance]: A total of 10 ppm precursor mass tolerance and 0.6 Da fragment mass tolerance were used in all data‐dependent analysis searches, and 10 ppm and 0.02 Da for targeted MS analysis in Proteome Discoverer.
Comment[FragmentMassTolerance]: A total of 10 ppm precursor mass tolerance and 0.6 Da fragment mass tolerance were used in all data‐dependent analysis searches, and 10 ppm and 0.02 Da for targeted MS analysis in Proteome Discoverer.
